news.headline: Ballard unveiled Q4 2024 results: $24.5M revenue (48% down from Q4 2023); $69.7M full-year revenue; $113M net order intake in 2024; $173.5M year-end order backlog; and $603.9M in cash for 2025. Initiating global corporate restructuring for a more than 30% decrease in 2025 total operating expenses. Q4 2024 saw improvement in gross margin, while total operating expenses and cash operating costs decreased by 5% and 6% from Q4 2023, respectively. Adjusted EBITDA improved to ($36.0) million from ($44.1) million in Q4 2023. Order backlog saw a 41% increase to $173.5M by the end of Q4 2024. 2025 outlook includes an estimate of $100-$120M in total operating expenses and $15-$25M in capital expenditure.
news.summary: Ballard Power Systems announced its financial results for Q4 2024, reporting a revenue of $24.5 million, down 48% compared to Q4 2023. The full-year revenue was $69.7 million, down 32%, with a standout performance in the Bus vertical showing a 51% revenue growth. The Q4 gross margin improved by 9 points year-over-year to (13%), while full-year gross margin was (32%). The company secured a new net order intake of approximately $113 million in 2024, ending the year with an Order Backlog of $173.5 million. Ballard achieved record shipments of fuel cell engines in 2024 and initiated a global corporate restructuring to lower Total Operating Expenses by more than 30% in 2025. Cash and cash equivalents were at $603.9 million at the end of 2024. Order Backlog for Power Products represented over 98% of the total, driven mainly by customers in Europe and North America. The company announced 2025 guidance with Total Operating Expense expected to be $100-$120 million and Capital Expenditure ranging from $15-$25 million. The revenue expectation for 2025 was back-half weighted due to the early stage of hydrogen fuel cell market development.

news.headline: Royal Gold issues guidance for 2025 sales, DD&A, and effective tax rate.
news.summary: In a recent announcement, Royal Gold has released guidance for the year 2025 regarding sales, depreciation, depletion, and amortization (DD&A), and the effective tax rate. The company has set forth its projections for these important financial metrics as part of its strategic planning. This guidance from Royal Gold is crucial for investors and stakeholders to understand the expected performance and financial standing of the company in the upcoming year. This information will aid in decision-making and assessing the potential returns on investment.

news.headline: Global heavy-duty truck market to hit US$ 950.71 billion by 2034, rising at 9.8% CAGR. East Asia leads, US$ 373.16b turnover in 2024.
news.summary: The global heavy-duty truck manufacturing market is expected to reach US$950.71 billion by 2034, with a projected CAGR of 9.8% from 2024 to 2034. Fleet managers are focusing on upgrading their vehicles to meet environmental regulations and lower maintenance costs. The market is driven by the demand for technologically advanced trucks offering increased reliability and reduced operating costs. East Asia dominates the market, with regions like South Korea and China seeing growth due to infrastructure projects. North America is estimated to reach US$53.14 billion in 2024. The short-distance freight operators segment is expected to grow at a CAGR of 9.4% from 2024 to 2034, with heavy-duty diesel trucks anticipated to reach US$230.8 billion in 2024.

news.headline: Advanced Flower Capital Inc. reports Q4 2024 GAAP net loss of $(1.0) million, but Distributable Earnings of $6.3 million.
news.summary: In the final quarter of 2024, there was a GAAP net loss amounting to $(1.0) million, equivalent to $(0.05) per basic weighted average common share. Additionally, there were Distributable Earnings of $6.3 million, calculating to $0.29 per basic weighted average common share.

"United Kingdom Furniture Market Outlook 2025 with profiles of 220 major UK furniture companies, analysis of market size and forecasts up to 2026, production, consumption, imports, and exports of furniture for the years 2019-2024."
news.summary: Dublin - A new report titled "United Kingdom Furniture Outlook 2025" has been recently included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings. This analysis provides insights into the UK furniture market, including key industry data and indicators. The report covers various aspects such as furniture market size, market forecasts until 2026, production, consumption, as well as imports and exports of furniture for the period 2019-2024. Additionally, data is segmented by different categories like upholstered furniture, office furniture, kitchen furniture, and other types of furniture. This comprehensive report aims to offer valuable information and projections regarding the UK furniture market.

news.headline: Space company Intuitive Machines (NASDAQ: LUNR) sees more price swings than BTC, down 61% since failed moon landing in Feb.
news.summary: Intuitive Machines Inc. (NASDAQ: LUNR), a space exploration company, experienced significant price volatility in 2024, surpassing the fluctuations seen in Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC). The stock went through a tumultuous year driven by headlines. Initially, LUNR shares surged to a two-year high in January amid optimism for its second moon landing attempt. However, the IM-2 lunar lander faced technical issues, resulting in a 250-meter miss from the target landing site. Subsequently, the mission was deemed unsuccessful, leading to a 55% drop in LUNR stock since the late February launch and over a 61% decrease in the recent period.

news.headline: Jeffs’ Brands reaches important milestone in merger with Fort Products Ltd, receiving valuation report of approximately US$11.6 - US$14.2 million.
news.summary: Tel Aviv, Israel, March 13, 2025 - Jeffs' Brands Ltd, a data-driven e-commerce company trading on Nasdaq under JFBR and JFBRW, has provided an update on the merger agreement between Fort Products Limited and Impact Acquisitions Corp. In a recent release, the company stated that Impact received a valuation report from Evans & Evans, Inc., assessing the fair market value of Fort Products' equity as between CAD 16.8 million to CAD 20.5 million (approximately US$11.6 million to US$14.2 million) as of January 31, 2025. This valuation was reported on a controlling, marketable basis, with specific assumptions and qualifications outlined by Evans & Evans.

news.headline: Turkey strengthens regulations for crypto, with new rules for exchanges and investors. Capital Markets Board overseeing compliance. Requirements set for CASPs. Impacts Turkey's crypto market. Aligns with global standards. Measures aim at stricter trading for investors. Restrictions for stablecoins and DeFi. Developing story. How crypto laws evolve globally in 2025.
news.summary: Turkey is strengthening its cryptocurrency regulations with the introduction of new rules for crypto asset service providers. On March 13, the Capital Markets Board of Türkiye released two regulatory documents outlining licensing and operational guidelines for CASPs, including exchanges, custodians, and wallet service providers. The framework gives the CMB full oversight of crypto platforms to ensure compliance with national and international standards. CASPs will need to meet strict requirements regarding compliance infrastructure and reporting, and will face challenges in adapting to the new regulatory environment. The regulations align with global standards like MiCA in Europe and the US SEC, and introduce stricter trading requirements for Turkish investors, potential stablecoin restrictions, and regulations on the decentralized finance market. The evolving situation will see further updates as more information becomes available.

news.headline: Build-A-Bear Workshop had record-breaking results in Q4 and fiscal 2024, with 0.8% revenue increase and 5.1% pre-tax income growth. Dividend raised.
news.summary: Build-A-Bear Workshop, Inc. reported its fiscal year 2024 fourth-quarter results, which ended on February 1, 2025. This period saw a 0.8% increase in revenues and a 5.1% increase in pre-tax income on a GAAP basis. It is important to note that this 13-week quarter was compared to a 14-week quarter in the previous year. Excluding the additional week in the previous year, revenues showed a substantial 5% increase.

news.headline: Ripple approved by Dubai regulator to grow in Middle East, targeting UAE's $40B cross-border payments industry.
news.summary: Having recently obtained a license in Dubai, Ripple is ready to access the rapidly growing cross-border payments sector in the UAE, which is estimated to be worth $40 billion.

news.headline: Milestone Pharmaceuticals reports Q4 2024 results; FDA PDUFA review goal for CARDAMYST™ on March 27, 2025. Approval may lead to launch for PSVT in mid-2025. Company had $69.7 million in cash by Dec 31 2024.
news.summary: Milestone Pharmaceuticals Inc. reported financial results for Q4 2024 and full year ending Dec 31, 2024. They aim for FDA approval for CARDAMYST™ nasal spray for Paroxysmal Supraventricular Tachycardia (PSVT) by March 27, 2025, with a proposed mid-2025 launch if approved. The company had $69.7 million in cash by Dec 31, 2024. They conducted a Commercial Launch Plan event and received a Notice of Allowance for a new patent. Milestone also plans to start a Phase 3 trial for etripamil in AFib-RVR in 2025. In 2024, the company had a net loss of $41.5 million. They are focused on developing innovative cardiovascular solutions like etripamil for patients with PSVT and AFib-RVR. Milestone Pharmaceuticals Inc. is traded on NASDAQ under the ticker MIST.

news.headline: Cellectar Biosciences achieved FDA alignment for approval of iopofosine I 131 for Waldenström macroglobulinemia.
news.summary: Reaches agreement with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) on regulatory steps for potential fast-tracked approval of iopofosine I 131 as a therapy for Waldenström macroglobulinemia (WM).

news.headline: Rail Baltica Estonia has awarded the construction contract for the Tallinn-Pärnu section to an alliance including AS Merko Ehitus Eesti and others.

This alliance, known as Part 1 or Alliance 1, was declared successful in the procurement process.

news.headline: Kinaxis named Carleton University's 2024 Co-op Employer of the Year, providing mentorship and skill development for students.

news.headline: Rockwell Automation unveils Emulate3D Factory Test at NVIDIA GTC 2025, offering virtual testing for manufacturers. #ROK
news.summary: In Milwaukee, Rockwell Automation, a leading industrial automation and digital transformation company traded on NYSE under ROK, is set to introduce its latest Emulate3D® Factory Test™ features at NVIDIA GTC 2025. This unveiling marks the first public display of the capabilities, showcasing how the tool facilitates large-scale virtual controls testing in factories. By leveraging this solution, manufacturers can effectively perform Factory Acceptance Testing to verify the functionality of automation systems prior to implementation. Visitors at the event will have a unique opportunity to witness the Factory Test in action.

news.headline: AdvizorPro releases 2025 RIA ETF Trends Report, detailing RIA ETF allocations in 2024. Includes fund adoption and issuer growth analysis.

This report offers a comprehensive evaluation of how registered investment advisors (RIAs) distributed their investments in ETFs in 2024. By utilizing public filings and AdvizorPro's exclusive analysis methods, the report presents unique market insights and detailed analysis of RIA ETF allocations. The 2025 RIA ETF Trends Report focuses on fund adoption, issuer expansion, and portfolio trends.

news.headline: Clarity AI introduces AI solution for sustainable research, reducing time by automating analysis for investment teams.
news.summary: Clarity AI, a prominent sustainability technology firm based in New York, has recently introduced an innovative AI-powered tool aimed at revolutionizing sustainability research for asset managers and investors. Through automation and the provision of immediate company information, this new solution empowers investment teams to break away from sluggish, unchanging service providers and gain complete autonomy over their sustainability evaluations. Analysts and portfolio managers, who often depend on disjointed information, antiquated third-party evaluations, and data.

news.headline: ImmunoPrecise Antibodies partners with biotech giant for cancer drugs, gets $8-10M; improves research with advanced technologies.
news.summary: In Austin, Texas, ImmunoPrecise Antibodies Ltd. (IPA) has revealed a new partnership with a prominent biotechnology firm with a substantial market value exceeding billions. The collaboration aims to propel the research and creation of Antibody-Drug Conjugates (ADCs) and bispecific antibodies to combat cancer. The partnership will combine the contracting research proficiency of both parties, along with IPA's exclusive B-cell Select™ platform and artificial intelligence technology. The strategic collaboration is expected to prompt advancements in the field of cancer treatment.

news.headline: AtkinsRéalis reports strong Q4 2024 results with record-high revenue. Services backlog at $17.2 billion, $10.36 per diluted share, $525.8 million operating cash flows for 2024. Outlining a positive 2025 outlook.
news.summary: AtkinsRéalis Group Inc., a global engineering services and nuclear company, reported strong financial results for the fourth quarter and full year of 2024. The company saw record-high revenue and Segment Adjusted EBIT across its various segments, with significant increases in operating cash flows and a record-high backlog for AtkinsRéalis Services. In the fourth quarter, AtkinsRéalis Services revenue was $2.5 billion, up 15.1%, while Nuclear revenue reached a record high of $464.3 million, up 67.0%. For the full year, AtkinsRéalis Services revenue hit $9.3 billion, up 16.3%, and Nuclear revenue reached $1.5 billion, up 42.6%. The company's full-year operating cash flows totaled $525.8 million. The firm is optimistic about future growth opportunities, with an outlook for 2025 projecting continued organic revenue growth in the Engineering Services Regions segment and strong financial performance in the Nuclear segment. The company aims to capitalize on its solid financial position to drive further value creation and growth in the coming year.

news.headline: Village Farms International reported Q4 and full year 2024 results. Full year sales hit $336.2M, with Q4 sales growing 11% to $82.6M. International sales increased 113% in Q4. Adjusted EBITDA impacted by $10.5M non-cash inventory impairment. Company broke ground on Phase II expansion in the Netherlands to quintuple production capacity. CEO optimistic about profitable growth initiatives in 2025. #VillageFarmsInternational #FinancialResults #GrowthInitiatives
news.summary: Village Farms International, Inc. reported a strong financial performance for the full year 2024. Consolidated sales grew by 18% year-over-year to $336.2 million. Canadian Cannabis sales also saw a significant increase of 31% year-over-year, with retail branded sales growing by 23%. The company held the #1 market share in dried flower, #2 in pre-rolls, and #3 overall market share in 2024. Full-year consolidated cash flow from operations reached $10.3 million.In Q4, consolidated sales grew by 11% year-over-year to $82.6 million. International sales increased by 113% year-over-year, and the company expects international sales to triple in 2025. Adjusted EBITDA in Q4 was impacted by a non-cash, non-flower inventory impairment of $10.5 million. The company also broke ground on a Phase II expansion in the Netherlands to quintuple annual production capacity.The company's President and CEO highlighted the positive trends in Canadian Cannabis sales, the growth opportunities in international markets, and the ongoing operational improvements across various subsidiaries. Despite the non-cash impairment impacting financial results, excluding this impact, adjusted EBITDA for Q4 and full-year would have been $7.0 million and $12.2 million, respectively.The company remains focused on establishing a global leadership position in the regulated cannabis industry and is optimistic about achieving profitable growth in 2025 and beyond.

news.headline: Lifetime Brands, Inc. reports Q4 sales of $215M, 6% YoY increase. TTM Adjusted EBITDA $55.4M. Launches Project Concord for intl growth; declares dividend.
news.summary: Lifetime Brands, Inc. reported fourth quarter sales of $215 million, a 6% increase YoY, with U.S. sales up 5.8% and international sales up 4.4% in constant currency. The Trailing Twelve-Month Adjusted EBITDA was $55.4 million. The company launched Project Concord for international business turnaround. Gross margin for the quarter was 37.7%. The company declared a quarterly dividend of $0.0425 per share. The relocation cost for the east coast distribution facility is estimated at $7 million. The Hagerstown Facility will require $10 million in capital expenditures. Full-year 2024 net sales were $683 million, a decrease of 0.5% YoY.Adjusted net income for 2024 was $12.6 million, or $0.58 per diluted share. The company announced an Investor Day in the fourth quarter of 2025. Non-GAAP financial measures include constant currency net sales and adjusted EBITDA. Lifetime Brands markets products under various well-known brands like KitchenAid and Mikasa. The Company's website is www.lifetimebrands.com. The full financial statement and balance sheet are shown in thousands. Forward-looking statements were mentioned in the press release, and contact information was provided.

news.headline: Medical Facilities Corp reported Q4 and FY 2024 results, including $96.1M from BHSH sale, $108.5M cash position, and $16.6M to shareholders.
news.summary: Medical Facilities Corporation reported its financial results for Q4 and year ended December 31, 2024. They sold BHSH for $96.1 million and received a net receivable of $0.7 million. Their cash position at year end was $108.5 million. They repurchased 1,700,700 common shares for $16.6 million and repaid $16.0 million on their credit facility. Revenue increased by 1.1% to $331.5 million for the year, with operating income up by 10.5% to $54.7 million. Adjusted EBITDA increased by 7.3% to $71.4 million. The president stated their focus on operational excellence and driving shareholder value.
